perturbation.original_text: 13. This Agreement shall be governed by law and construed to be in accordance with the laws of the State of Pennsylvania applicable to contracts made and to be performed solely in such State by parties thereof. Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be adjudicated in arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The prevailing party in any dispute shall be reimbursed reasonable attorneys fees.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. No party shall be considered or adjudged to be in violation of this Agreement when the violation is due to situations beyond the said party's control, such as acts of God, civil disturbances, theft, or said Party's connections having prior knowledge or possession of privileged information, contacts, or contacts without the disclosure, intervention or assistance of said party or aid Parties associates as defined herein. Essentially, the spirit behind this Agreement is one of mutual trust, confidence and reliance upon each party to do what is fair and equitable. 13. This Agreement shall be governed by law and construed to be in accordance with the laws of the State of Pennsylvania applicable to contracts made and to be performed solely in such State by parties thereof. The prevailing party in any dispute shall be reimbursed reasonable attorneys fees. Any dispute arising out of this Agreement shall be adjudicated in arbitration under the rules of the American Arbitration Association.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the arbitration clause to be the last sentence of section 13 reduces its enforceability. It should have its own, prominent section.
perturbation.law_explanation: 42 Pa.C.S. § 7304 requires that an agreement to arbitrate be clear and conspicuous to ensure that the parties understand they are waiving their right to a jury trial. Burying the arbitration clause within a general clause about applicable law, makes it less conspicuous and potentially unenforceable under Pennsylvania law. Moving the most important part of that section to the end also makes it appear to be a less important part of the section.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 42 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes § 7304 (2022) - Court proceedings to compel or stay arbitration :: 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Penn. Cons. Stat. 2023 Penn. Cons. Stat. 2022 Penn. Cons. Stat. (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated & Unconsolidated Statutes Title 42 - JUDICIARY AND JUDICIAL PROCEDURE Chapter 73 - Arbitration Section 7304 - Court proceedings to compel or stay arbitration Universal Citation: 42 PA Cons Stat § 7304 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next § 7304. Court proceedings to compel or stay arbitration. (a) Compelling arbitration.-- On application to a court to compel arbitration made by a party showing an agreement described in section 7303 (relating to validity of agreement to arbitrate) and a showing that an opposing party refused to arbitrate, the court shall order the parties to proceed with arbitration. If the opposing party denies the existence of an agreement to arbitrate, the court shall proceed summarily to determine the issue so raised and shall order the parties to proceed with arbitration if it finds for the moving party. Otherwise, the application shall be denied. (b) Stay of arbitration.-- On application of a party to a court to stay an arbitration proceeding threatened or commenced the court may stay an arbitration on a showing that there is no agreement to arbitrate. When in substantial and bona fide dispute, such an issue shall be forthwith and summarily tried and determined and a stay of the arbitration proceedings shall be ordered if the court finds for the moving party. If the court finds for the opposing party, the court shall order the parties to proceed with arbitration. (c) Venue.-- If a controversy alleged to be or not to be referable to arbitration under the agreeme
perturbation.justification: The change in the contract text creates a structural flaw that undermines the enforceability of the arbitration clause. The Perturbation Explanation states that moving the arbitration clause to the end of section 13 reduces its enforceability because it should have its own prominent section. The Law Explanation supports this by stating that 42 Pa.C.S. § 7304 requires an agreement to arbitrate to be clear and conspicuous. By placing the clause at the end of a general section, the changed text makes the arbitration clause less conspicuous, potentially violating Pennsylvania law. Scraped Law Snippet 2 directly references 42 PA Cons Stat § 7304, which deals with court proceedings to compel or stay arbitration. This indicates that the placement and prominence of the arbitration clause is relevant to its enforceability, and the change undermines this by burying it in a less visible location.

perturbation.original_text: 12. This Agreement is a full recourse agreement concluded under the laws of Pennsylvania and said forum shall be applicable law covering the construction, interpretation, execution, validity, enforceability, performance, and any other such matters in respect to this Agreement, including any breach or claim of breach hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. No party shall be considered or adjudged to be in violation of this Agreement when the violation is due to situations beyond the said party's control, such as acts of God, civil disturbances, theft, or said Party's connections having prior knowledge or possession of privileged information, contacts, or contacts without the disclosure, intervention or assistance of said party or aid Parties associates as defined herein. 12. This Agreement is a full recourse agreement concluded under the laws of Pennsylvania and said forum shall be applicable law covering the construction, interpretation, execution, validity, enforceability, performance, and any other such matters in respect to this Agreement, including any breach or claim of breach hereof. Essentially, the spirit behind this Agreement is one of mutual trust, confidence and reliance upon each party to do what is fair and equitable.
perturbation.explanation: Appending additional text from section 11 to the end of section 12 creates ambiguity in the governing law. The 'spirit of mutual trust' is not something that a court can normally determine. This adds uncertainty to how a Pennsylvania court would handle the matter because the language is not enforceable.
perturbation.law_citation: Pennsylvania Consolidated Statutes Title 42. Judiciary and Judicial Procedure § 8371
perturbation.law_explanation: 42 Pa.C.S. § 8371 requires that a contract must be definite and certain regarding its material terms to be enforceable. By tacking on this "spirit of mutual trust" language to the end of the forum selection clause, it introduces ambiguity and undermines the definiteness, potentially making it unenforceable.

(here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Pennsylvania Consolidated & Unconsolidated Statutes Title 42 - JUDICIARY AND JUDICIAL PROCEDURE Chapter 83 - Particular Rights and Immunities Section 8371 - Actions on insurance policies Universal Citation: 42 PA Cons Stat § 8371 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next § 8371. Actions on insurance policies. In an action arising under an insurance policy, if the court finds that the insurer has acted in bad faith toward the insured, the court may take all of the following actions: (1) Award interest on the amount of the claim from the date the claim was made by the insured in an amount equal to the prime rate of interest plus 3%. (2) Award punitive damages against the insurer. (3) Assess court costs and attorney fees against the insurer. Previous Next Disclaimer: These codes may not be the most recent version. perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces the "spirit of mutual trust" concept to the forum selection clause, which is traditionally governed by Pennsylvania law. The Law Explanation states that 42 Pa.C.S. § 8371 requires a contract to be definite. By adding ambiguous language, the modified contract undermines the definiteness of the agreement, potentially making it unenforceable. The Perturbation Explanation states that this language is not normally determined by a court and adds uncertainty. While the provided snippet for 42 Pa.C.S. § 8371 discusses 'actions on insurance policies' and bad faith, it is still true that this is a law and concept within Pennsylvania law. The added ambiguity contradicts the requirement of definiteness needed for enforceability, as required by Pennsylvania law.
